title: Pterostylis extranea
intro: Pterostylis extranea, commonly known as the Greenhood orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to eastern Australia. The Greenhood orchid is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pterostylis extranea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pterostylis extranea is often used to prepare tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Pterostylis extranea is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Pterostylis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids found in Australia. The classification of Pterostylis extranea is further broken down into the family Orchidaceae, order Asparagales, and class Liliopsida. This classification places Pterostylis extranea within the larger group of flowering plants. The species is often referred to as a terrestrial orchid due to its growth habit.
